item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's now an example domain, a little help here? What was it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is not a child or even a human.

It's a trap.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What kind of interactions would she have with say SCP-999 or SCP-096?

Would 096 regenerate?

Would she end up killing 999? I don't think that's possible anyway, as its kind of a blob of goo. Plus I think violence is completely against 999's nature.

EDIT: If 999 is actually younger than 053, then I guess 999 would just want to play with her. My belief is her effect only applies to those older than her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Isn't this the second oldest surviving SCP?

If so, wow.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think the second: Her effects continue to only work on people older than her. As she grows, her effect continues to affect those who are her age or older than her.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
People older than three attack her. She's three.

When she gets older, will she kill herself? Or will her effects continue to only work on people older than her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems they were referring to this

item.title: Re: Image Removal
item.content:encoded:
R.I.P. Original image of SCP-053.
Ya kinda freaked me out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She was born in 1987?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She was born in 1897?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+420. Nice.

Sweet and short Series I skip. I don't think it would pass today, but it's a classic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The scips that happen to be children are always sad to me.
They could have had a normal, happy childhood and life until the Foundation took them. Very sad.

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded:
O_OLagMasterO_O

This is the second time you've been staffposted for replying to comments that are nearly 9 years old.

In this case you are even aware of this but decide to do it anyway despite the fact you've been told not to before.

Don't do this again.

This is a closed staff post. Do not reply unless you are staff.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: old comment I know but still gonna reply lol.. it does say on the scp that the girl is generally always happy so I doubt it needs affection as it also has higher brain functioning so it isn't as if it's a little child that needs safety blanket.. at least that's how I read it 😂😂

item.title: Re: Image Removal
item.content:encoded: Dang all of my favorite SCP's are getting their picture removed

item.title: Image Removal
item.content:encoded: Image was found to originate from an image rental site. Picture removed, future update may include a replacement. Apologies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm also wondering how 053 and 191 would be. I figure 134 would be just about the same, since all one has to do is be near 053, so lack of sight would make no difference.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: Both articles are based around the idea of an object/creature causing people to want to either destroy it or kill it. The link suggests that both are connected in some way.

item.title: Re: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: I can't figure out how the two SCPs would be connected to each other, even if they have similar effects.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I too am curious at what the original 611 can be considering I was confused when I read it and less confused when I came back to see your response.

There should be an archive here.

Of all places that should have archives it's the Foundation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ok… So I know this comment is almost ten years old, and that JoystickHero has not been active for about seven years, but can anyone give me an explanation for what 611 Hero is referring to? The current 611 was created in 2010 (So what Hero is referring to must have been deleted or moved) and the Old wiki and Wayback Machine both come up empty for 2008 or older… Also if I am just missing something obvious then I am truly sorry for being an imbecile. Thank you for your time.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This will forever be my absolute favorite SCP. I love the idea, I for some reason feel empathy for this fictional character. I love everything about it.

item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded:
Timer Expired: Edits below have been made. All WikiWalk team edits may be reverted by the original author at any time.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 72 hours if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
- hide block

Both SCP-053 and SCP-1129 effect individuals once visual contact has been established, they both also cause individuals to become increasingly paranoid, and attempt to harm the scip itself. Due to these similarities and the brevity of SCP-053, I'd like to work a link into this passage instead of changing the present content.

Editing this passage:

Any and all humans over the age of three who make eye contact with, physically touch, or remain around SCP-053 for longer than 10 minutes will rapidly become irrational, paranoid, and homicidal. Most, if not all, of these feelings will be directed at SCP-053, and afflicted subjects will attempt to kill SCP-053 after first killing or driving off all humans visible to them.

To read:

Any and all humans over the age of three who make eye contact with, physically touch, or remain around SCP-053 for longer than 10 minutes will rapidly become irrational, paranoid, and homicidal. Most, if not all, of these feelings will be directed at SCP-053, and afflicted subjects will attempt to kill SCP-053 after first killing or driving off all humans visible to them.

While there are several portions of this passage that could contain the link to SCP-1129, I felt as if having it at the point that the similarities become more abundant would be the best fit.

If anyone has other/better examples that could be used here please leave a comment saying so!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I've always liked this one.

I do wonder if the age cutoff is developmental or literal. That is to say, if a "copy" of an adult human with the knowledge/comprehension of the original had been made through the use of another SCP (I'm certain there are a couple with that effect but I'm blanking on numbers), would it still experience the compulsion if it had come into existence less than four years ago?

I'd read that tale. Or write it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Added to my headcanon1: people who try to kill her do so because they hear her singing the map song in their heads XD

Footnotes
1. Just kidding

item.title: Re: Questions and more questions! :)
item.content:encoded:
I'm trying to establish just what is or isn't affected by her. 682 for some reason wasn't, so which SCPs aren't affected? Are human-based SCPs affected or not? If one of them is affected and attacks her, will they die? It could help pinpoint the exact cause of her hate field and what it affects or not.

I really doubt 173 would successfully kill her, if nothing else has at this point. All it does is break people's necks, and so far we've seen nothing that suggests it does any supernatural damage.

Also, 173 and 96 are easy to handle so long no one blinks or looks at the face respectively. They went through the trouble to shove 862 onto her cell, just to see what would happen. I don't see how a giant human-hating immortal reptile is easier to handle than an evil statue that only moves when no one is looking and a whimpering beast that is docile and harmless if you put a mask or bag on its head from behind. It would be much easier to just have her follow a crayon path.

Besides, I wasn't saying they SHOULD do it. I was just wondering if they already had or not.

item.title: Re: Questions and more questions! :)
item.content:encoded:
I would advise against cross-testing this one, if the Foundation was a real thing. While they don't know why it does the things it does, they know WHAT it does, which is enough to see the potential for danger.

Especially showing 173 to this little girl… why? 173's not even human… so he might actually have a chance of killing her successfully, though I doubt it. Not to mention the difficulties in recovering 173 from 53's chamber.

And if 173 is hard to cross-test, how the hell are they going to manage 96?

item.title: Questions and more questions! :)
item.content:encoded:
I wonder, has SCP ever questioned people who stay with her and leave before the 10-minute limit is broken? What do they say? Is the aggression sudden and without any previous effect, or does it build up over time in the form of paranoia and annoyance? Also, what is the exact area of her hate plague effect, how far away from her is it safe? If restrained via darts or other means before they can harm her and die from heart attacks, do they ever calm down and return to sanity, or is the effect permanent?
Also, I understand that she seems ignorant/amnesiac after each attack, but how does she react when the attack does happen? Does she seem surprised, scared, calm, apathetic?

What is 053/April generally like? What does she do while in isolation? Is she happy, shy, playful, mysterious? Is she chatty or quiet? Does she eat, sleep, cry, play with toys, watch TV(Does she even have a TV in her cell?)? Does she complain about being lonely or bored? The file only says she is generally pleasant. Also, the file isn't clear, is Abby still growing or is she non-aging?
Also, I was reading the "Parenthood" file. Has SCP ever looked into this "head injury" incident? It seems the incidents started one month or so AFTER she hit her head? There IS the possibility this could be some latent psychic ability gone out of control, or maybe some sort of alien parasite that entered her head through the wound and causes this "hate field". Sounds crazy, but what doesn't in this place?
Also, the file indicate that 53/Abby's Father went berserk, but only after a few days, whereas the indication was that her mother wasn't affected as badly. Has the Foundation detected any sort of increase in the area and potency of the "hate field"? The father's last ramblings also spoke of how the mother was "infected". Did SCP conduct any autopsy? Did it show anything unusual? If the mother displayed some sort of "immunity" that could at very least lessen the effects of this hate field, it would help mantaining the girl without risking our people going insane after 10 minutes.

And finally, I understand that, somehow, 682 has taken a liking for the girl. The idea itself is scary, as is exposing any child to any of the most dangerous SCPs, but for the sake of argument, has the Foundation conducted more experiments exposing other SCPs to 53, such as 173, 49, 56, 96? How do they react to her? Are only humans affected by her?
Or even, and preferrably, do we allow her to interact with the other, harmless SCPs, including the children her age, such as 40, 191, 131, 134, 163, 208, 662? Is there any risk, especially of Euclid-class disaster?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After reading the description, and the results of what happened when she was photographed with the Desire Camera, I'm wondering if she might not be an incarnation of St. Martha.

If you read the legend surrounding her, Martha could dominate (in the mental subjugation sense) anyone and anything. In fact, it goes on to talk about her assisting a town in France by subjugating a dragon (known as the Tarrasque) by tying it up. With Ribbons.

This leads me to the possibility that the desire to kill her is because she's not got her Domnation power under proper control, and thus the homicidal urges people get around her are actually a form of self-defence; people subconsciously know that she can control them, and thus they try to kill her to prevent it happening?

Might be complete rubbish; might not. Either way, it's an interesting hypothesis that fits the available data.

item.title: Re: Passive-aggressive
item.content:encoded: What if anyone wore 714? Would she freak out at the zombie that comes at her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, if it had to die ASAP, would it work to let her run into a mine? or would the one that set the mine die?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Old post, i know, but this should be more obvious in the article. Im left feeling sorry for the little one, while i should be terrified of it.

item.title: meh...
item.content:encoded: This one's pretty boring.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wonder what that kid in the photo would think if she saw this article as an adult to find that she's a rage-inducing monster.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This'n holds up pretty well for Series I, I think. Short, scary, and lets your mind wander with the possibilities. And for once, the crossover in the 682 test log enhanced the quality of the piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because if she's just kept away from people, nothing really happens. Object classes are based on ease/difficulty of containment, not potential for danger.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: why isn't she a Keter class? Put her in the middle of the city and chaos would insue

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The fact that she's 3 years old and that anyone her age and younger is not affected is interesting. If she ages like a human, then wouldn't the age "limit" go up as well?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is good, and it has aged well. It's sad, it's creepy, it's believable. My only problem with it is the nagging question (which keeps coming up in discussion) of how long she has been 3 years old. No, that's a serious question. It matters. You don't have to tell the reader, and I would recommend against doing so, but the Foundation does know, and it should be noted. If she has been observed to not age, there absolutely 0.00% chance that that wouldn't have been noted in the notes. If this has not happened, it would be assumed that she does age, and that means that saying "3 years old" without a date stamp is about as useless as talking about the left side of a sphere.
In short, if she doesn't age, you damn well better put in a footnote because that's a really noteworthy anomaly. That would be anomalous enough to contain even without other effects.
If she does age (or if we haven't noticed yet, since she's only been in containment for ██ months) then it should say that she "appeared to be roughly three years of age as of her containment on ██-██-████." Or 12-17-████, or whatever.
It matters because it would matter to the researchers for whom this is written. They have to plan for long-term containment. It will matter to them when she hits puberty, when she turns 21. It will matter how long she's been in containment and how she has developed and whether and how her effects have changed.
If this is meant to be a snapshot of her right after recovery, fine — more than fine, even! Great! But it should say that. Why? Because the Foundation insists on things being properly documented, and saying "she's three" without listing a year is like saying "the left side of the mushroom" without saying where the front is or where you are.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I couldn't stop laughing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thank you… which image do you mean? Well, the girl of image that I put is cute too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having the girl herself be anything but innocent-looking would be detrimental to the article. It runs on the contrast between her nature and her effect. The picture is good.

E: And you don't need to use brackets to make external links clickable.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't know why, but the girl of the image shouldn't be showing her face, it would give a darker touch to this SCP, here is an example:

http://i.istockimg.com/file_thumbview_approve/9208829/2/stock-photo-9208829-sad-little-girl-looking-down-and-holding-flowers.jpg

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Care to elaborate on that?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This SCP should be updated, in content and maybe a new image…
Whoo! 100 upvotes!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
YES

I wish I could upvote this

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or maybe, like Boa said, 053 is something so beyond our understanding that the concepts of life and death doesn't apply to it. Maybe, Mr. Lizard didn't react to "her" simply because it didn't perceive her as a living creature at all.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Maybe the only reason that 682 didn't kill her was because she's also a member of his… species? group? home planet? dimension?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hit the nail on the head. The brain knows there is something WRONG with that "little girl", and pretty much forces you to try and get rid of it. However…something about that moment, when you actually "kill" her, closes a switch in the brain…and you're suddenly exposed to…whatever. Either it kills you, or your body just stops to prevent something worse from happening…and the girl goes skipping along.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I didn't like this one all that much, but then I read what Gears said and I really started liking the idea that she's not a human child, she's some eldritch abomination from beyond the void that wears the disguise of a child and its mere presence is enough to drive us mad. This girl shaped 'thing' is so alien and beyond our scope of understanding that its very being drives us over the edge, we don't have to know what it is because what it is so incomprehensible that it breaks our minds. That's why we try to kill it, our brain doesn't know what's wrong, it can't know what's wrong but it knows it's the girl and it knows she needs to die right now before her presence destroys your mind.

That's why I like 053 now, not because she's an immortal brat but because she's a little girl who's not a little girl at all. Maybe the form of a little girl isn't what it looks like at all and it's just the closest thing our mind can comprehend (like the color magenta but instead of a color it's a physical impossibility beyond the constraints of sanity.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I wouldn't want to kill her if she'd just stop singing the "I'm the map" song from Dora the Explorer for ONE GOD DAMNED MINUTE

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What always messed with me about this one was… well… the possibility that she was. Human. Child. Little girl.

And Gears said...

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't think she has parents.

I don't even think she's a she. Or a child.

Or human.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If he's such a weenie he couldn't have conceived 053 now could he?

item.title: Re: Experiment
item.content:encoded: We don't want to risk something happening to 073.

item.title: Re: Passive-aggressive
item.content:encoded:
Humm…sounds like I may have to add some logs or something…I think she's not a bed wetter, but as for the skin cell question…humm…

The Telekill might actually work…but I have to wonder how she would react. maybe she freaks out, like some kind of…allergic reaction to it.

item.title: Passive-aggressive
item.content:encoded:
What happens if we stop feeding her?

Or add poison to her food?

(I know, I know. She's highly-voted enough that we're not gonna decommission her. But what would happen?)

Also - since she at least seems human, she wears clothes. Clothes get dirty and need to be washed. Are any of her skin cells on those clothes? What happens to the water that's used for doing the laundry? Since she looks and acts three, is she a bedwetter?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I know…but, I mean, even I can't be creepy all the time…

item.title: Re: Experiment
item.content:encoded: I say we introduce her to SCP-073. Just to see what happens.
